message:
fix(core): handle btrfs xattr hash collisions in setxattr and removexattr

Btrfs stores xattrs keyed by a CRC32 hash of the attribute name, so
hash collisions are possible.  Previously setxattr blindly overwrote the
entire item payload and removexattr deleted the whole B-tree key,
silently destroying neighboring xattrs that shared the same hash slot.

Extract three helpers:
- btrfs_serialize_xattr_item: encodes a single name+value pair into the
  on-disk dir_item format.
- btrfs_merge_xattr_item: reads the existing payload (if any), updates
  or appends the target entry, and re-serializes all entries.
- btrfs_remove_xattr_item: filters out a single entry by name; returns
  an empty vec when the last entry is removed so the caller can delete
  the key, or a trimmed payload to update in place.

setxattr now merges into existing payloads instead of replacing them.
removexattr now surgically removes only the target entry, preserving
siblings.

Add btrfs_write_xattr_hash_collision_preserves_neighbors test that
verifies set, update, remove, and listxattr all behave correctly when
two xattr names collide on the same hash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
